agent_instruction: Use data.context.entries before opening card files. Open full_card_required paths only when present. Combine bounded context with prospect, use the routed CTA and output rules when present, and do not invent claims outside the loaded context.

entries.body: Owns AI search visibility, answer-engine share of voice, citations, prompt tracking, and the transition from classic SEO reporting to AI-generated answers.
entries.title: AEO Lead

entries.body: Profound helps brands understand and improve visibility in AI-generated answers across answer engines, with platform surfaces for insights, prompt volumes, agents, shopping, and agent analytics.
entries.title: AI answer engine optimization platform

entries.body: This pack represents Profound's GTM decision layer: target slices, buyer personas, fit gates, evidence-backed claims, hooks, CTAs, avoid-rules, and gaps before any downstream copy or outreach tool acts.
entries.title: Seller-side decision pack

entries.body: The first slice is marketing teams and agencies that already have SEO, content, PR, brand, agency, or infrastructure signals showing they care about discovery, citations, sentiment, and traffic attribution.
entries.title: Target slice, not total market

entries.body: MDP stores the context contract and routes cards for agents. It does not send messages, enrich leads, update systems, or run campaigns.
entries.title: MDP is not execution

entries.body: Use when the account or person has SEO, AEO, organic growth, demand generation, or search ownership and needs visibility, citations, prompt tracking, or share-of-voice insight across answer engines.
entries.title: Good fit: AEO or SEO ownership

entries.body: Use when the account manages multiple brands, client workspaces, markets, languages, prompt sets, reporting needs, or answer engines where a reusable AI visibility platform matters.
entries.title: Good fit: agency or enterprise complexity

entries.body: If the row lacks persona, segment, trigger, signal, and source context, return insufficient-context instead of forcing a recommendation or writing copy.
entries.title: No fit decision without context

entries.body: Disqualify rows where the account has no meaningful web, content, brand, PR, search, ecommerce, agency, or AI visibility surface for Profound to evaluate.
entries.title: Bad fit: no public marketing surface

entries.body: Disqualify requests that ask MDP itself to scrape, sequence, send, enrich, or update external systems. Produce a handoff boundary instead.
entries.title: Bad fit: execution-only ask

entries.body: Use when the account has enough AI-search marketing surface area to turn answer-engine visibility, prompt volumes, citations, crawler data, or competitive gaps into prioritized projects, briefs, and agent workflows.
entries.title: Good fit: AI search opportunity operations

entries.body: Disqualify or route to manual review when the only evidence requires login, private social access, personal contact enrichment, paywalled content, or access-control bypass.
entries.title: Bad fit: private-source-only evidence

entries.body: Do not qualify or append a ledger row unless the account trigger is paired with a named public person/role owner at the same company. Account-only evidence may become a gap or manual-review candidate, but not a qualified lead.
entries.title: Require public person-role evidence before qualification

entries.body: Strong persona signal when the title or source row includes SEO, AEO, organic growth, search, demand generation, or visibility ownership.
entries.title: SEO or AEO ownership signal

entries.body: Technical fit signal when the source row mentions server logs, AI crawler traffic, attribution, cloud/edge platforms, CMS workflows, or analytics ownership.
entries.title: AI crawler and infrastructure signal

entries.body: Strong account signal when public or supplied context mentions rising AI-search prompt demand, high-value prompt sets, category comparison queries, topic trends, or intent research for AI conversations.
entries.title: Prompt volume opportunity signal

entries.body: Strong signal when the source row shows the team needs to convert many AI visibility gaps into prioritized projects, execution briefs, agent handoffs, and measured outcomes.
entries.title: Aim project operations signal

entries.body: Source-quality signal when the candidate row includes a public unauthenticated URL, title, observed date or freshness marker, bounded snippet, provider family, and confidence score.
entries.title: Public-source ready signal

entries.body: Strong source-quality signal when the candidate includes a named person, current role/title, company match, public profile, author, or team URL, bounded role snippet, provider family, and confidence score tied to the same account trigger.
entries.title: Public person-role resolution signal

entries.body: The buyer cannot see how often the brand appears in AI answers, how it ranks against competitors, or which prompts and topics matter.
entries.title: AI search blind spot

entries.body: The team needs to understand which pages and domains AI engines cite, where competitors are winning, and which content or earned-media opportunities matter.
entries.title: Citation and share-of-voice gap

entries.body: The team does not know which AI systems are crawling the site, how content is interpreted, which pages matter, or whether AI-driven activity connects to traffic.
entries.title: AI crawler attribution gap

entries.body: The buyer does not know which AI conversation topics, prompts, prompt volumes, or intent patterns should drive tracking, content, or earned-media prioritization.
entries.title: Prompt demand blind spot

entries.body: The team can see many AI-search signals but lacks a repeatable way to rank opportunities, create execution briefs, hand work to agents, and measure impact.
entries.title: Signal to project gap

entries.body: Profound helps brands understand, analyze, build, and measure their presence across AI-generated answers and answer-engine discovery surfaces.
entries.title: Profound AI answer engine optimization

entries.body: Answer Engine Insights tracks AI visibility, mentions, citations, sentiment, competitive benchmarking, platform comparisons, and trends across answer engines.
entries.title: Answer Engine Insights visibility

entries.body: Profound Agents support content refresh, AEO FAQ generation, competitive research, net-new content creation, no-code workflow building, brand-kit context, human approval steps, and CMS integration.
entries.title: Profound Agents content workflows

entries.body: Agent Analytics tracks AI crawler activity, content interpretation, traffic attribution, page performance, benchmarking, and infrastructure-level signals using server-log and crawler-visibility workflows.
entries.title: Agent Analytics crawler attribution

entries.body: Profound's public pages describe enterprise compliance/security capabilities such as SOC 2 Type II, SSO, RBAC, encryption, and GDPR compliance.
entries.title: Enterprise compliance/security

entries.body: MDP can store GTM messaging decisions in a manifest plus modular cards so agents route source-backed fit, persona, claim, CTA, avoid-rule, and gap context before drafting.
entries.title: MDP vetting context

entries.body: Prompt Volumes helps teams identify the keywords, topics, prompt demand, trend lines, and intent signals driving AI conversations before choosing what to track or create.
entries.title: Prompt Volumes demand intelligence

entries.body: Aim surfaces ranked AI-search marketing opportunities, turns accepted opportunities into projects and data-backed execution briefs, hands work to Profound Agents, and tracks impact.
entries.title: Aim marketing agent projects

entries.body: For the first LinkedIn touch, use one public or supplied signal, one fit hypothesis, and one low-pressure owner or relevance question. Do not imply the prospect has intent or that Profound measured their current visibility.
entries.title: LinkedIn initial touch

entries.body: Create a brief with mdp --json brief --context after a prospect row has public-source evidence and before any copy is drafted. Save the JSON artifact and, when needed, render the readable Markdown brief for operator review.
entries.title: Brief command handoff

entries.body: If the target owns search or organic growth, frame the hook around knowing where the brand appears, which prompts matter, and where competitors are winning in AI answers.
entries.title: Answer-engine visibility hook

entries.body: If the target has analytics or infrastructure signals, frame the hook around seeing which AI crawlers access the site and which pages connect to AI-driven traffic.
entries.title: AI crawler attribution hook

entries.body: If the target owns AEO, content, or agency reporting, frame the hook around knowing which AI prompts and topics are worth tracking before investing in content or visibility work.
entries.title: Prompt volume hook

entries.body: If the target already has AI visibility signals, frame the hook around turning those signals into ranked projects, execution briefs, agent handoffs, and measurable follow-through.
entries.title: Aim project hook

entries.body: Default to asking who owns AI search visibility, AEO content, brand narrative in AI, or client AI visibility reporting before asking for a meeting.
entries.title: Owner validation ask

entries.body: Ask whether AI answer visibility, citations, or AI crawler attribution is already on the team's roadmap. Keep the ask small and falsifiable.
entries.title: Relevance check ask

entries.body: When the source context supports it, offer a Profound demo or AEO report as the next step. Do not imply a custom analysis has already been run.
entries.title: AEO report or demo ask

entries.body: Do not manufacture urgency, claim the account is falling behind, or imply the prospect asked for help unless the source row explicitly says so.
entries.title: No false urgency

entries.body: For scout handoffs, ask the operator to run or review an MDP brief before drafting outreach. Recommended local command: mdp --json brief --dir examples/profound-gtm-vetting --prospect examples/profound-gtm-vetting/examples/profound-aeo-scout-row.json --channel linkedin --job "linkedin outbound copy" --context.
entries.title: Create brief before copy

entries.body: Do not describe the decision pack as a sender, CRM, sequencer, enrichment provider, scraper, BI tool, AI SDR, or generic automation system.
entries.title: Do not claim execution

entries.body: Do not claim the target brand has low AI visibility, negative sentiment, missing citations, or active buyer intent unless the source row includes that evidence.
entries.title: No unsourced prospect visibility claims

entries.body: Do not add quantified lift, guaranteed rankings, guaranteed traffic, guaranteed citations, or revenue claims unless the exact proof is in the approved claim context.
entries.title: No unsupported outcomes

entries.body: Do not collect, infer, buy, expose, or invent personal contact data. Public role/persona fields are routing context only.
entries.title: No contact harvest

entries.body: Avoid panic language about being erased by AI or falling behind. Use source-backed hypotheses and questions instead.
entries.title: No fearmongering

entries.body: Do not use em dashes in generated copy. Use commas, periods, colons, or shorter sentences instead.
entries.title: No em dashes

entries.body: If the user or pack states a paragraph count, match it exactly. Do not add setup, recap, or explanation paragraphs outside the requested structure.
entries.title: Honor paragraph count

entries.body: Do not explain why the copy works, describe the structure, or include drafting notes unless the user asks for critique or rationale.
entries.title: No meta commentary

entries.body: Use crisp, monochrome, product-led language for operator artifacts. Prefer concise headings, tables, bullets, and exact command blocks over decorative prose, purple-brand language, or card-copy flourish.
entries.title: Plain Vercel-style output

entries.body: Return target account, target persona, segment, fit decision, observed signals, likely pain, approved claims, CTA rule, avoid-rules, gaps, and recommended next step.
entries.title: Brief contract

entries.body: For outbound, structure copy as one observable signal, one fit hypothesis, one Profound-relevant pain, and one owner or relevance question.
entries.title: Signal -> hypothesis -> question

entries.body: Use the create-brief path as the default operator handoff: public source evidence -> normalized prospect JSON -> mdp --json brief --context -> readable brief -> human-reviewed copy or CRM sync.
entries.title: Brief command pattern

entries.body: For scout outputs, preserve the chain from source-strategy target to provider result to accepted evidence to MDP fit/brief decision to normalized ledger row. Every material claim needs an evidence id or gap.
entries.title: Source strategy to ledger pattern

entries.body: The pack can identify a plausible target slice, but it does not know the prospect's current answer-engine visibility score, citation share, sentiment, or prompt performance unless supplied.
entries.title: Missing prospect visibility data

entries.body: A title or persona guess does not prove who owns AI search, AEO content, PR narrative, or agency reporting internally. Ask an owner-validation question.
entries.title: Missing current owner

entries.body: Do not assume the prospect uses a specific CMS, analytics setup, edge provider, or approval workflow unless the row supplies that context.
entries.title: Missing stack and workflow proof

entries.body: The extracted pricing page showed plan structure but not reliable exact numbers, so this example does not quote exact prices.
entries.title: Pricing not quoted

entries.body: MDP can model target-account decisions and produce a brief, but it does not perform external execution or prove production Profound strategy.
entries.title: MDP boundary

entries.body: Before deploying live scouting, the operator must approve provider allowlists, query budgets, rate limits, evidence retention, and whether Exa, Firecrawl, Apify, CRM, or Slack connectors are enabled.
entries.title: Provider allowlist gap

entries.body: When a source shows account-level AI-search fit but no public named owner, role/title, company match, and source URL, preserve the account trigger as a person-resolution gap and do not invent a contact.
entries.title: Missing person resolution receipt

context.policy: Use context.entries first. Treat entry metadata as advisory context, not enforced CLI constraints. Open full_card_required paths only when present, or when the user asks for a full pack/card audit.

runtime_context.local_time_policy: MDP emits UTC runtime metadata only. If a workflow needs fiscal year, renewal date, event date, campaign window, or local business calendar logic, pass that as reviewed pack-declared metadata or supplied source context.

decision_trace.reason: use supplied prospect/account JSON as task input
map person title to pack persona
load only relevant message decision cards
use the brief as the agent/model input contract

draft_decision: Proceed with routed brief using stated assumptions.

provider_family.description: Optional reviewed provider family such as exa, firecrawl, apify, or manual.

source_strategy_id.description: Optional reviewed MDP source-strategy id used to discover this row.

segment.description: Pack-owned Profound GTM segment labels accepted from normalization prompts.

source_kind.description: Provider-neutral source markers accepted from normalization prompts.

fit.decision: Proceed to route/brief with stated assumptions.

matches.title: Good fit: AEO or SEO ownership
Good fit: PR and brand narrative
Good fit: agency or enterprise complexity
Good fit: AI search opportunity operations
Good fit: agency AI presence practice

prospect.background: Synthetic public-source row for the Profound GTM pack. The account has an organic growth owner, a public content surface, and a stated interest in understanding AI answer visibility, prompt demand, and citation opportunities.
prospect.name: Jordan Lee

signals.title: Prospect title indicates organic growth and search ownership
Account context mentions interest in which AI prompts and topics drive category discovery
Account has a public content and resource-center motion
Public person-role page names Jordan Lee as Director of Organic Growth at ExampleMarket Co

prospect.title: Director of Organic Growth
prospect.trigger: Account appears to have SEO, content, and organic growth ownership that could make AI answer visibility and prompt demand worth exploring

prospect.background: Synthetic public-source row for the Profound GTM pack. The account has an organic growth owner, a public content surface, and a stated interest in understanding AI answer visibility, prompt demand, and citation opportunities.
prospect.name: Jordan Lee

signals.title: Prospect title indicates organic growth and search ownership
Account context mentions interest in which AI prompts and topics drive category discovery
Account has a public content and resource-center motion
Public person-role page names Jordan Lee as Director of Organic Growth at ExampleMarket Co

prospect.title: Director of Organic Growth
prospect.trigger: Account appears to have SEO, content, and organic growth ownership that could make AI answer visibility and prompt demand worth exploring

prospect_source.guidance: Synthetic example fixture. Replace with a real or intentionally sanitized prospect row before production use.

route.description: Target buyer personas Profound wants to reach and what each buyer needs to decide.
Claims, category drift, execution boundaries, and tone that agents must avoid.
Global style, formatting, and output-structure rules for generated Profound GTM text.
ICP, fit, disqualification, and no-message rules for Profound target-account vetting.
Profound category, market slice, boundaries, and MDP role in the GTM workflow.
Buyer pains, triggers, stakes, and why the account may need Profound.
Observable account and persona signals that make Profound relevant.
Reusable Profound message hooks after fit and evidence checks.
Approved Profound claims and proof requirements an agent may use.
Reusable message and brief structures for Profound GTM outputs.
CTA rules, reply paths, and ask boundaries for Profound outreach.
Channel-specific policy for LinkedIn, email, call prep, and agent briefs.
Known gaps and open questions agents must surface instead of filling in.

runtime_context.local_time_policy: MDP emits UTC runtime metadata only. If a workflow needs fiscal year, renewal date, event date, campaign window, or local business calendar logic, pass that as reviewed pack-declared metadata or supplied source context.
